_doc: Daylight scoring rules. A room 'has daylight' if at least one of its polygon edges collinearly overlaps an exterior outer polygon edge (outer_is_exterior=true) within overlap_tolerance_m. Only habitable rooms are evaluated; non-habitable rooms are ignored in the aggregate.

room_rules._doc: Keys are room_type strings. 'habitable': true = included in daylight evaluation; false = excluded (not scored, not counted in aggregate).

Bedroom._doc: Habitable — must have exterior wall access.

Living room._doc: Habitable — must have exterior wall access.

Kitchen._doc: Habitable — must have exterior wall access.

Children 1._doc: Habitable — must have exterior wall access.

Children 2._doc: Habitable — must have exterior wall access.

Children 3._doc: Habitable — must have exterior wall access.

Children 4._doc: Habitable — must have exterior wall access.

Hallway._doc: Circulation space — does not require daylight.

no_habitable_rooms_score._doc: Apartment-level daylight score when zero habitable rooms are present (e.g. only WC+Bathroom). 100 = vacuously true (no habitable rooms to fail). Set to 0 to treat the absence of habitable rooms as a failure instead.

overlap_tolerance_m._doc: Max perpendicular distance (meters) between a room polygon edge and an exterior outer polygon edge for the overlap to count as valid daylight access. 0.01 = 1 cm.

aggregate._doc: How to aggregate per-room daylight pass/fail into a single score. 'mean_pass_rate' = (habitable rooms with exterior wall / total habitable rooms) * 100.
